reviews.review_text: Great park. Excellent location. Wasn't planning on travelling through Quorn it was a last minute change of plans. So glad our plans had changed and were able to experience this beautiful town.
Absolutely fabulous park, simple clean plenty of hot showers and wonderful staff. Great camp kitchen and laundry facilities
Great location for Taylor in caravan park
Great park on the way to the Flinders Ranges.
A good clean friendly park with excellent facilities good friendly staff short walk to town.
Lovely quiet park set amongst mature eucalyptus. Very peaceful.

A very big thank you to the wonderful Clint and Karen for our amazing time at the Quorn Caravan Park.
They are a fabulous team who made our reservation and stay so easy.
It is a wonderfully cared for park, with a beautiful location, that is so relaxing, so enjoyable and perfectly positioned for all of what Quorn and the outstanding Flinders Rangers has to offer.
Again, very big thank you 🙏.

Stayed over Easter. A typical outback caravan park with friendly and professional owners who take a lot of care in making the park as nice as possible and they achieve this admirably. Just across the road from the famous Pitchi Richi railroad, and a small outback town with 3 pubs to choose from and a great silo laser show right out the front!

We stayed in a standard ensuite cabin for just one night. We were greeted by a really welcoming staff member who took time to ensure we had information that we needed.  I have to admit the cabin looked tired and dated from the outside but as they say don’t judge a book by its cover! On entering it was clean and fresh. Yes the decor is dated but everything worked well. The bed was comfy and the shower lovely and hot. An easy walk into town for sightseeing.
Great caravan park close to town and silo light show. Shady sites and clean amenities. Friendly staff

A great family run caravan park.  Amenities were excellent and great facilities for campers.  Reasonably priced.  Spotlessly clean and I would stay there again.  Very quiet, but you may be woken early from the birdlife and good facilities in the nearby township.
Nice and quiet place to have a rest from the road
Nice place, facilities very clean, lovely staff
It was an alright caravan park. Alot of bugs and mozzies.
Super clean and well maintained caravan park. Walking distance to the quaint little town of Quorn. Go and see the series of short films projected on the silos each evening after dark

Stayed 3 nights in RV section. Option of shade or open sun with plenty of space to choose from. Very tranquil and quite location. Facilities are very nice plus "hot"  showes. Staff are most helpful with good local knowledge and of the surrounding area. I would definitely come here again if in the area, or even detour to make use of it.

I can't rate what the park was like inside, but when I rang 2 hours prior to arrival I was told for a cabin, ( 2 adults, 2 kids ) $240 a night for 1 night or $215 a night for 2 nights. On our 10 day holiday, Pt Augusta ,Coober Pedy, Mt Dare, Oodnadatta, William Creek, Marree. This was the most expensive, but I agreed on the phone. Upon arrival I was told an extra $25 surcharge on top of original quote. While I was disputing the $25 I could see her friendly attitude changing quickly.  She wouldn't budge from original quote so I left and found a nice clean ensuite room 2 minutes away for $160 per night. Being 4pm on a Tuesday ( not school holidays ), I presume no one took the cabin that late in the afternoon / letting $430 walk out the door from trying to chase an extra $25. Thankyou, you saved me $135.

This is such a nice Caravan  Park  and the second time we have stayed there. Friendly  staff, always happy to assist. The facilities are clean and showers lovely and hot. This really is a nice place to stay

We absolutely loved our stay in Quorn.
Booked a site for one night, but extended it for three.
Great space, clean, neat, tidy, friendly.

What a fantastic park. Spacious, level sites. Very quiet at night. Super clean amenities. A great laundry with fairly new machines, and they have 'borrow' baskets and pegs to use if you need to.
Super friendly staff and just a short walk to town for a great meal at the pub.
We will definitely stay again when in the area.
Thank you Quorn 👍🏼.
Great little caravan park in very central location. Staff were amazing and site was spacious

Very bad mannered people. Got send away and told we were not able to stay here after we said we would go into town first. Very rude. Especially since there are not many other options in town. Ended up finding something out of town that was cheaper and way nicer! Thanks for showing us the door
Rude staff. Wouldn’t recommend staying here. Such a waste of the nice town of Quorn.
Rude people. There are much nicer parks outside of Quorn.

Very well located a short walk across the railway lines to the town centre. A choice of shady sites or sites backing onto the greener edges of the park. Good facilities with shower pressure and hot water.
A lovely park with clean facilities and good showers. Very hot and dry but close to the hotel for dinner, straight across the railway line. Perfect to stay hete and go on the Pichi Richi steam train.

So much to like about this place from the steam train to the silo light show or just walking the town.
make sure you go to Scruffy Fellas for a coffee and a quandong tart.

Quorn caravan Park is an excellent park and a great location to walk everywhere.|Amenities very clean and a high standard, with the park well maintained.|With the train station 1 minute walk and also the light and sound show on the silos very good entertainment
A pleasant stay was had in one of their cabins. Clean and tidy,  fitted with the essentials. A quiet park close to town with pathways to walk and enjoy the local surrounds.

Quainr little caravan park in a lovely area. The staff were helpful and friendly. Site was big. The light show on the silo was really interesting and gave a good summary of the local area. Showers and toilets were spotless.

Nice location
Clean well kept park
Facilities all one could ask for
Minor complaints
Poorly allocated sites tents in large sites which would be perfect for long caravans, while a 8.5 mtrs van had a small site and tricky to get into when park is full. Saw this within a few sites.
Grey water run off from site 12 to 15  ran down hill into sites 24 to 29. Sites 24 to 29 had to try and run grey water uo hill.
We used a bucket and emptied in grey water drain.
U and a few others in our row felt it was not very hygienic. Hate to be allocated these sites if I was camping in swag or tent!
Besides those two issues, had a good stay.
We would stay again as long as we don’t get sites 24 to 29.
Well worth the 2 night stay.

What a beautiful way to start our Flinders Ranges adventure. The park was clean and quiet, had clean amenities and Clint and Tarryn the managers were very pleasant and helpful. We stayed in one of the two-bedroom onsite cabins and found it to be quite comfortable. After having a wander around the park and seeing their camp kitchen, shower and laundry block and the choices of caravan sites (powered, unpowered etc ) my wife and I decided next time we will bring the van up and stay for a week. Been to many parks and would rate this up there as a great country town park. Thanks guys!

Helpful staff, clean facilities, great camp kitchen and walking distance to town.
Love their dump site.   A toilet with seat and flush.   After emptying your container in the toilet you flush.   Fabulous funny idea.   Well done who thought of that.
Enjoyed our stay.

We stayed at a unpowered campsite with our car+ rooftop tent and really liked it. After a long drive, we arrived later than the Office hours, but that was no problem, as they already prepared an evelope for us. We could find everything we needed in there.
The facilities are well maintained and super clean. I can recommend the Caravan park for everyone :)
Lovely clean convenient caravan park on the Flinders Ranges Way between Adelaide and Marree. Dog friendly. Very clean amenities. Plenty of bird life.
Lovely caravan park that is located just behind the main street. Easy access just across the old railway lines. Clean bathrooms and good laundry. Would definitely stay there again.

Wow, what a terrific park.
Very friendly owners who actually work in the park.

Very helpful if you have a problem. Let us checkout later as,we had to fix our caravan, no issues at all.

Great working washing machine with detergent already included

Very clean park, well looked after

Dumppoint on site
Water and power
Large sites - (no sardines feeling)
Easy check in

Lots of tourist info at reception, brochures nicely arranged on rack - just amazing
Map for guidance in caravan park

Lovely walking tracks around the park

Dog friendly,  10min walk to an amazing dog park

Quorn, quickly little town with supermarket, cafes and pubs, walking distance

Best caravan park in SA
Nice park great location
Well run and clean park. Here for the races and visiting Warren Gorge, Devils Peak and the Silo light show

We stayed in Quorn 3 days.
The history, buildings people, views, food, walks, gorges, drives, silo night projection, pubs, IGA the list goes on were all fantastic.

A very quiet and spotlessly clean caravan park. So quiet we slept late and had to rush to get away the next day.
The laundry has three new washers and a dryer. $5 load but has self dosing detergent. There are pegs and laundry baskets for guests to use also.
It was a convenient stroll across the railway tracks to town and the beautiful Quorn Silo light display at night. A real shame we only stayed 1 night could have definitely stayed here longer.

Great Caravan Park. They would win the award for the Cleanest Caravan Park in Australia! It is so nice to go to a Laundry and the machines are clean 1st time for me! Highly recommend recommend stay here and such a credit to the owners!!!
We have stayed here before. It is a rustic park but all facilities are good. the service is excellent and the prices are reasonable.
Good park with good facilities and location

Most affordable, clean and very accommodating.
Very happy staying here

Rang 5 minutes to 5 (closing time) and they were so accomodating to letting us have a really nice spot even though they were full. Recommend this place even if your passing through or need a few days relax and you pick quorn then I would suggest you stay here :) showers/toilets are good.

Thanks for allowing us to change our booking a couple of times due to getting hws repairs done in PA.
Great friendly staff.
Very basic
Access is via keypad on thin post near boom gate just inside main gate. Contact them before going to pay and get the code. Huge grassy area.
Great little stop on the way into the Northern Flinders. Very clean facilities 👌

Cabins are very clean and well stocked with everything you need.
Clint and Tarren are very friendly and welcoming. Would highly recommend
Great place to spend a couple of days

Loved this park. For a wee country town it is fantastic. Amenities were clean and spotless. Nice to sit around the campfire each night talking to to fellow travellers. Good size bays. Pity the drought made everything a wee dusty. Make sure you watch the Silo show at sunset
Great van park on the middle of town . Right on top of the Silo laser light show and the Pichi Richi Rail  .

Fabulous park. The sites are big. The ablution blocks are clean & adequate. The hosts are so nice & very accomodating. It is an easy walk into town where the shops are really good. Special mentions to Scruffys & The Quandong Cafe.|Just loved it. Also easy access to some fabulous 4x4 tracks & incredible scenery
Very dry, facilities OK. Good place to on a weekend when the tourist train is operating and the Silo picture show on nightly

Everything was very clean and spotless, lovely surroundings among the gum trees. We only stayed for one night at the campsite so don't know about accommodations. Caravan park is a short walk to silo night show.
One of my favourite caravan parks around! Great open kitchen and services!!

We are interstate travellers moving all around the state. We have been in lots of caravan sites and it was joy to have the best shower today.
Most caravan sites don't seem to realize that people like to undress and dress in a dry area.
Good shower draining and dry area to dress in are much appreciated.
Great park. Lots of room. Clean amenities and grounds

Lovely friendly service and good overnight stay. Great food across the way at the trans Continental hotel. A few mosquitos about but they seem to be around more in the day and disappear at night. Amenities and facilities located lovely and clean. Do not expect grass but trees around 🙂

The MOST tidiest amenity block you will ever come across. Friendly staff.
Well kept camp sites. Would return hands down !
Great place to stay. Spacious sites. Clean amenities with hot showers. Staff very friendly.
Nice caravan park with upgrades ongoing. Good communal facilities. The town makes the most of what it has in the area.
Great caravan park - every thing is very clean and tidy..  the night silo show is very well done and the locals at the pub are friendly and up for a good yarn..  great spot to call through 👍

Quorn caravan park ,so relaxing quite place very clean bathrooms lovely camp kitchen, Great price for power sites .The owners Clint and Tarran lovely people well done to them regards to them Teena Mark and Billy the dog
Caravan Paek is set up in a Bush atmosphere.  Beautiful and peaceful.  Clean, camp sites are spacious.  Great place to stay.
Really well kept place and near to the SiloArt light show.
Great service, and for a standard cabin in a caravan park they were very tidy. The cleaners here are very meticulous.
Nice little park, unfortunately for us a big storm hit and we lost all power. Not the parks fault by any means

Powered Van site , Quiet Country Caravan Park , Dirt and gravel sites , Were parked along way from the clean camp kitchen even though we booked 4 months in advance , amenities very clean and a 1/2 Klms walk into what’s left of town ,
Excellent service.  Location was a plus , walking distance to the pub and the Silo light show .

Lovely caravan park, the host was lovely, checkin was easy, the toilets were sparkling clean and the sites were large. There is also walking paths nearby, including the local footy oval and the bush tucker trail.  We would definitely stay here again.  The Flinders gin distillery is a must do and if u want awesome coffee and cocktails you need to visit the Scruffy Fella Cafe.

We camped ina powered site, fatalities were clean and well equipped
Camp kitchen looks great we didn't use it but could see others did

CP is a little gem. New owners having a good go, improving things along the way.
Amenities are old but spotless. Un-powered site allowed us to stay hooked up overnight. Quiet location. Can do the herritage walk from the back gate around into town and back and call in to the silo cinema on dark.
Highly recommend this CP
Superbly run. Excellent position. Just walk across the rail tracks to the silo pics and town!
A really nice caravan park.
We stayed 2 nights very a great place to stay. The staff were very friendly and helpful. Will definitely be back next time we are travelling though.
Walking distance to the town and lots of heritage places interesting shops and cafes and the Pichi Richi 🚂 to have a great day /weekend etc
Stayed in an unpowered site.  Quiet and peaceful.  Handy location to walk into town.  Amenities clean.  Friendly staff.

We recently camped at the Quorn caravan park for 2 nights.  The manager was very friendly and helpful.  The park is situated in a quiet location, and not far  from the shops and pubs. All the amenities were very clean.
Own accommodation facilities very good, location excellent very short walk to main street.
Nice spot big bays
Had a powered site with not quite enough room for the camper and the car. Site next to me seemed to need a part of my site.
Great place
Very quiet, but good facilities and plenty of space. Be careful of the trees next to the road though.

Fabulous little caravan park. Amenities are kept spotlessly clean and the staff are very friendly and responsive to any requirements.
Laundry ablution block and camp kitchen are all well equipped and the grounds are beautifully maintained.
On our last weekend of an extended stay, we were lucky to be here for the Steamfest which was right across the road at the Quorn railway station. Beyond that is the lovely historic town of Quorn. Easy walking access to the IGA supermarket and 2 pubs for a quick pint or a meal.
Thank you for a wonderful stay.

Accommodation options in Quorn are limited and the cabins here are old and basic but comfortable enough for a short stay. Book the hotel for dinner as it is busy every night with workers in town. Odd metal bed is bolted to the floor but mattress firm and comfortable. Good shower.
Very clean and well managed park
A lovely park, well maintained and with all the amenities you need.  Quorn CP and town are wonderful gems.

Super friendly staff here and the park is within a short 5-minute walk to the main streets. Drive-thru sights book fast but there are ample sized powered and unpowered bush block sights. The laundry is small but the appliances are excellent and washing lines aplenty. We visited twice within a 2 week period and will definitely stay here again.
Lovely run caravan park on the edge of the Flinders Rangers. Super easy checking and Easy drive thru powered sites. Enjoyed the walking tracks as well as the Silo light show.

We stayed at the QCP for one night.
The caravan park is beautiful and clean, it backs on to walkways and park land so we could walk our dog.
The staff were ever so friendly when we pulled up and gave us all the information we needed.
Amenities were lovely and clean, showers were piping hot and water pressure was lovely, a nice way to warm up on a cold winters night.
Would definitely stay here again if we travel through.
Highly recommend
Just a very nice and quiet caravan park. Walking distance to shops and a great light show on the silos.
We stayed 3 night o a powered site , the park is very well presented and a lovely place to pull up, we would highly recommend.

Just a one night stay here on out way back south. Awesome location, quiet with great ,clean amenities.
Probably the most different dump station I have seen😀

Stayed for a week on a powered Site with our 40ft Bus and absolutely loved it. Everything was so clean and tidy and the managers are very nice! 😊
We will be back! 😊

We stayed one night. Quorn is a neat, attractive, and quiet town - so a great place for a stopover. |Our powered site was big . Sites seemed well spaced in amongst the trees - not " cookie- cutter " rows. |We used the camp kitchen  - basic but clean.||Shared facilities  were old but spotlessly  clean and maintained.  ||No hesitation  recommending  - especially  if you are looking for somewhere  well away  from the noise of PtAugusta .
Great park. Lots of amenities kettle, toaster BBQs.

An amazingly beautiful caravan park in a wonderful town.
The amenities are spotless and there is an excellent camp kitchen.
Very short walk to centre of town.
The nicest people managing the park. So helpful.
Highly recommend

Thank you to Tarran and Chris for the help you gave us when we had problems with our kitchen sink.
We very much appreciate the good service that you provide for your customers.
We look forward to another visit when in the area. 👍
Lovely caravan park right on the edge of the town, able to walk to the light show. Cleanest facilities I've seen.

Great little park in a great little town. Stayed here twice on our visit to Flinders Ranges. Two nights on our way up and three on the way back. Friendly welcome and staff. Very clean amenities. A walk across the train line and you’re in town. Walking distance to the nightly silo light show. The Pichi Richi railway trip is worth doing. Highly recommend this park

Quorn is a beautiful small town full of charm. Visited the Gin distillery and was amazed at how great it was. Silo films at sunset was a highlight of our stay. Pitchi Ritchi railway also a great attraction. We had a meal on a Monday night at local pub that was packed.
Would thoroughly recommend the stop at Quorn.

What a gem of a town! The Quorn Caravan Park is in a great location, has fabulous spotless facilities and friendly staff. Highly recommend putting this town and caravan park on your itinerary in South Australia.
Wonderful helpful staff & clean amenities, a great place to base yourself to explore the Flinders
Friendly bush park and owners.  There is plenty of room.

Sehr zentral und doch sehr ruhig gelegener Platz mit sehr netter Leitung.
Dusch- und Toilettenbereich sehr sauber. Transcontinental Hotel mit sehr gutem  Pub und Essen 3 Minuten zu Fuß
Awesome place to stay foe a home away from home.

A great little stopover on the way to or from the Flinders Ranges. Rustic sites (under new management from 1.3.24) with clean and tidy amenities. Close to the nightly light show on the silos. Three town also has a lot to offer visitors.
An amazing caravan park, heaps of shady spots for caravans and tents. Excellent amenities, easy to get in to town. Great spot to stay, thanks.

Thoroughly enjoyed this Caravan Park. Great, clean facilities, incl. laundry with ironing-board. Clint was very welcoming even though we were a little late arriving. The silo light show was very well done and nice to see Quorn doing something other than painting their silo. |The camp kitchen is beautiful with the solid timber support poles and roof. Solid floor, comfortable chairs and tables & BBQ! A great balance of enviroment and space per site. Top site, highly recommend.

Julie very helpful and informative about the area and its main features. We were in a motor home. Park facilities wonderful. We were there principally for the train journey into the Flinders. The free light show is walking distance from the park. We stayed an extra day to explore the railway shops and amazing mountain tracks outside of town.

Quorn Caravan Park offers an excellent $25 tent site in the breathtaking Flinders Ranges. The location is truly picturesque, providing a nice spot with ample room for a comfortable camping experience. The affordable price makes it a great choice for budget-conscious travelers seeking a serene escape.
Additionally, the Quorn Caravan Park impresses with showers boasting good water pressure, adding a refreshing touch to the overall camping experience in the stunning Flinders Ranges.

Just stayed for 3 nights in a lovely shady spot with a garden next to our site. Amenities were spotless and the staff/owners were amazing, so friendly and helpful. Quorn is a great little town, would recommend dinner at the Transcontinental Hotel and scones at the tea room.
Great quiet place friendly staff .. been 2x and will be back 🙂

We had a fantastic stay at this park for 3 days. The owners were welcoming and helpful. The amenities were spotless. It is a small park with a friendly atmosphere and the  attention paid to the environment is fabulous. My green heart loved it.
Nice tree's caravan park with good facilities.
Good location close to town. Good facilities and services.

Lovely caravan park.  It is rustic with many trees and saw a few stumpy lizards. Camp kitchen has small electric burners, kettle, microwave, 2 big BBQ and comfy table and chairs.  It was a quiet site when we were there.  The toilets and showers were clean and very good water pressure.  Only a short walk to see the silo show which was well worth seeing.  It was a cool night so we were able to stay in the car and tune in to the commentary.  Nice clean town and would go again.

If you arrive at 18 h, the office is closed
And when you phone, the answer is, we are close!

Great carvan park, friendly staff and quiet. Great surroundings.
Must go & see the Silo light show at night, its beautiful.
The old town is beautiful & lots of history.

Cabin srupulously clean including sparkling windows. Well maintained. Much appreciated after some indifferent cabins in larger towns.
Owners lovely to deal with being very helpful with tourist and local information. Efforts to improve the park with an eye to ecological sustainability is paying off. Thankyou.
Friendly service and spotlessly clean. Some bbq utensils would be good for touring cyclists.

One of the nicest caravan parks we have been. It’s bushy style and cleanliness are a credit to the managers and staff. Quorn itself is such a hidden gem and we fell in love with the place. The gin bar is a must in the afternoons. If you’re considering staying, just do it.

The staff here are so friendly and it is a lovely place to camp with an excellent camp kitchen. Very easy access to the Quorn Silo Lightshow which is an absolute must!|Quorn is a rare gem of a town and this campsite is ideally located just across the road from the railway.
Awesome park.  Nice a quite, will definitely be back.

We turned up at 9:30 in the morning as our plans changed at last minute and the owner was EXTREMELY RUDE. I was happy to not check in yet but he wouldn’t even let us leave the camper and come back and the place looked empty. He did not care that it was HOT and had two young kids in the car. Went to the RV park down the road. Would definitely not stay here!!

Scored a site for 4 nights, wanting to catch up with laundry  tidy and clean van  catch a steam train ride and get vehicle serviced. We managed all of above with some help from Bronwyn
got the vehicle serviced. Nothing was too much trouble. Amenities  and Laundry were spotless. I

Amazingly clean facilities given the current dusty environment.  Great staff.
We had a powered caravan site.
Great place to relax after a long day's travel. There is a walk, and close enough to visit the town.
Stayed in our caravan here, lovely caravan park with very clean amenities. 3-4 minute walk to the streets

Great park, clean and tidy ,spent 5 days there, would thoroughly recommend.
The manager and staff were terrific, nothing too much trouble, explained points of interest in the area and how to get there.5 minute walk to town,  Transcontinental hotel serves a great meal and friendly atmosphere
An absolute gem of a park. the amenities were clean and well cared for. the sites were large and spacious.  The gardens were well maintained.  It is a short work to the main street.
Very peaceful,  amenities fantastic. Definitely stay again

Unfortunately they pack you in like sardines.
Otherwise a great spot

We really enjoyed our stay in the park in our caravan. The park has a lovely natural & rustic feel and the amenities are spotlessly clean. The managers are very friendly,  helpful & knowledgeable about the local area.
The location is great, just a short walk to everything in the township, including the Pichi Richi Railway.
The caretaker here is an absolute champ!

Would not recommend. Had a booking for 2 nights. Payment in full was needed to secure our stay,  (paid as wanted the booking). Initially gave 6 weeks notice of cancellation, gave all info including bank details, then waited and waited for refund. Followed up on refund, again giving all necessary info. Checked bank and did not receive full refund. Made contact to query and was informed that there was a $30 cancellation fee. This information was NOT given on our original emailed confirmation of our booking, it was NOT informed when the booking was cancelled, nor was it informed when the follow up was made. A park that needs payment in full for the duration of a stay, has an uninformed $30 cancellation fee,  and had to be chased up for a refund does not deserve any stars, but could not post unless at least 1 star given. After this issue I looked at their website, and info can be found there, however as a returning customer we had the phone number and did not need to visit the website. Have never been to a park that does not disclose cancellation policy on booking confirmation. Very very disappointed. Will NOT be returning to Quorn Caravan Park
Friendly, helpful, very clean, excellent location, great surroundings, would stay at this awesome caravan park in a heartbeat!  Quorn is a great example of a progressive country town!
